name: Wobbun
concept: A handmade plush rice-cake creature, permanently dented by one gentle thumbprint it never quite rounded back out of, with sweet red filling glowing softly through its tummy.
imagePrompt:
A small plush rice-cake creature standing upright and facing the viewer, full body, head-to-toe, no legs to speak of — just one soft rounded mass.

SILHOUETTE (clean, closed, chunky): a single broad, settled dome — slightly wider than it is tall, like a ball of dough that has just relaxed onto a table. The bottom is flat and heavy where it sits; the top rises into ONE thick pinched twist of dough, a fat little top-knot swirl (chunky and solid, at least as wide as an eye — never thin or wispy). Two short stubby pinched arm-nubs bulge from the sides, rounded like mittens with no fingers. Two small flat foot-nubs peek out at the base. Everything is one connected body: no floating pieces, no strands, no separate ears, no accessories.

THE ONE IDEA — the thumbprint: high on the upper-left of the dome, a soft, shallow, clearly-drawn dent, exactly the shape of a thumb pressed once into fresh dough. It reads as a permanent, tender dimple that lifts that side of the face into a gentle, quizzical little tilt. Soft rounded shading inside the dent; the surrounding surface bulges kindly around it. It is asymmetrical on purpose and it is the creature's whole personality.

PALETTE: overwhelmingly one colour — a warm creamy blush pink-white (like milk with a drop of strawberry), matte and floury. Against it, one bold accent: a soft, rounded, glowing azuki red — deep berry-crimson — visible as a warm rounded patch low on the tummy, as if sweet filling is showing through the thin skin, its edges softly blurred outward into pink like light through fabric. That red patch is the only saturated colour on the whole creature. Shading in dusty rose; highlights in warm off-white.

MATERIAL — plush, unmistakably: brushed velour / minky fabric with a soft napped fuzz reading as gentle, even velvet texture (not loose fibres — no fringe, no fluff sticking out of the outline; the outer edge is smooth and definite). A raised stitched seam ridge runs like a soft equator around the lower body where the two fabric halves were sewn together, with visible chunky topstitching. The top-knot has a spiralled fabric pucker. A fine pale starch dusting reads as soft flocking across the shoulders, with one slightly shinier, well-loved worn patch on the belly where it has been hugged too often.

FACE (low on the dome, close together, honest): two big round glossy black bead eyes like sewn-on buttons, each with a single clean white highlight and a smaller secondary glint. Small warm rose blush ovals under each eye. A tiny simple mouth in dark thread — a soft, contented, slightly upward stitched curve, a single embroidered line. No nose. No eyebrows. The expression is calm, patient, quietly hopeful — a face that expects to be looked after and has decided to be fine either way.

PROPORTIONS: baby-chunky. Head and body are the same single mass. Eyes sit at roughly the lower third. Overall weight sags gently toward the flat bottom so it feels soft, heavy and squeezable.

RENDER: thick clean dark-plum outline of even weight, soft rounded ambient shading, big simple readable shapes, nothing finer than a chubby stitch, no text, no symbols. Warm, sturdy, huggable.
